meta.title: Mercedes GLS450 GLS500 GLS580 Engine Replacement Cost UK
meta.description: Every Mercedes‑Benz GLS450 / GLS500 / GLS580 engine — M256, M278 and M176. X166 and X167 generations, real UK costs and reliability. Get free quotes from 25+ vetted Mercedes specialists today.

@graph.name: Mercedes‑Benz GLS450 / GLS500 / GLS580 Engine Era Map
Mercedes‑Benz GLS450 / GLS500 / GLS580 Engine Codes

mainEntity.name: How much does a Mercedes‑Benz GLS450 / GLS500 / GLS580 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned engine typically costs £3,000–£12,000 supply only depending on generation: M278 (£3,000–£6,500), M256 (£3,500–£7,500), M176 (£5,000–£12,000). Fitting adds £1,000–£1,800. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

mainEntity.name: Is the Mercedes‑Benz GLS450 / GLS500 / GLS580 engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: It depends on the engine. The M256 (GLS450) is widely considered the most reliable — owners report 0% engine issues at nearly 100,000 miles. The M176 (GLS580) has known exhaust clamp and oil leak issues but low complaint rates. The M278 (GLS500) has timing chain tensioner and oil leak issues. The M256 is the recommended choice for reliability.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Mercedes‑Benz GLS450 / GLS500 / GLS580?

acceptedAnswer.text: M176 exhaust clamp leaks (check engine light, rough running), M176 oil leaks, M278 timing chain tensioner rattle, M278 cam magnet / cam sensor oil leaks, and M256 engine harness issues. All are repairable if caught early.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Mercedes‑Benz GLS450 / GLS500 / GLS580 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value and generation. Against a well‑kept X167 GLS580 worth £30,000–£60,000, a £5,000–£12,000 recon is a manageable 8–40% of the vehicle's value — worth it for a well‑maintained example. For an X166 GLS500 worth £10,000–£20,000, a £4,500 recon is more marginal.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Mercedes‑Benz GLS engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. The GLS450 uses M256 (X167 2019–present); the GLS500 uses M278 (X166 2015–2019); the GLS580 uses M176 (X167 2019–present). Tell us your registration date and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the M176's exhaust clamp leaks, or does it need a new engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — exhaust clamp replacement is a straightforward repair costing £200–£600. Mercedes TSB LI07.61-P-073583 addresses this issue. One‑piece clamps must be replaced with two‑piece clamps. This does not typically require engine replacement.

eraMap.title: The GLS450 / GLS500 / GLS580 THROUGH TIME — Era Map

rows.eraNote: The 4.7 V8 in the first-generation GLS — timing chain rattle, cam magnet oil leaks and coolant pipe failures are the main watchpoints.
The modern 3.0 straight-six with 48V mild hybrid — refined and generally reliable; engine harness and starter issues are the main watchpoints.
The twin-turbo 4.0 V8 — smooth and potent; exhaust clamp leaks, oil leaks and misfire issues are the main watchpoints.

replacementCosts.h2: Mercedes-Benz GLS450 / GLS500 / GLS580 Engine Replacement Costs
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a GLS petrol engine typically takes 14–18 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer). The hot‑vee layout of the M176 and M278 requires significant disassembly.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: Mercedes-Benz GLS450 / GLS500 / GLS580 — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Replace with two‑piece clamps; TSB LI07.61-P-073583 covers this
Valve cover, oil cooler or oil pan gaskets; catch it before low oil damage
TSB LI05.1o-P-056435 covers this; catch it before chain jumps

rows.problem: M278 cam magnet / sensor oil leaks (GLS500)
rows.whenItMakesSense: Preventative — catch it before oil reaches the ECU
Replace coolant lines and hoses — common failure
Wiring harness inspection and repair; less common than other issues

buyingChecks.title: Buying This GLS Petrol? Check These First
buyIf: * X167 GLS450 with the M256 inline‑six — considered one of Mercedes' most reliable modern engines
* M176 GLS580 with documented exhaust clamp and oil leak repairs
* M278 GLS500 with documented timing chain tensioner work (TSB completed)
* Full Mercedes service history — all GLS engines are sensitive to oil quality
avoidIf: * M176 with check engine light and no diagnosis — potential exhaust clamp or misfire issue
* M278 with cold‑start rattle and no timing chain tensioner history
* Oil in camshaft sensor connectors — potential ECU damage risk (M278)
* Poor service history on any generation — V8s and modern turbo engines are unforgiving of neglect

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The GLS450 / GLS500 / GLS580 badge spans three very different engine families. The M256 (GLS450) is widely considered the most reliable choice — a modern straight‑six with 48V mild‑hybrid that owners report as bulletproof. The M176 (GLS580) is a potent V8 with known exhaust clamp and oil leak issues but relatively low complaint rates. The M278 (GLS500) is the oldest of the three, with well‑documented timing chain tensioner and oil leak issues. Buy the M256 if you want the most reliable option; buy the V8s if you want the power but demand documented service history.

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Mercedes‑Benz GLS450 / GLS500 / GLS580 (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Engine Type — Confirm which engine the car has (M256 for GLS450, M176 for GLS580, M278 for GLS500) — see the Era Map above
* Cold Start — Listen for rattling noise on startup (M278 timing chain tensioner warning)
* Check Engine Light — Any CEL? Could be exhaust clamp leaks on M176 or other issues
* Oil Leaks — Check for oil spots under the car or burning oil smell (M176/M278)
* Cam Sensor Connectors — Disconnect and check for oil — oil in the plug could mean a new ECU (M278)
* Misfire History — Any rough running or misfire codes? Early M176 production had misfire issues
* Engine Harness — Any electrical issues? GLS450 engine harness problems have been reported
* Service History — Full Mercedes history with documented oil changes
